L1.area: Server-side service layer templates and utility modules for code generation

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where security-sensitive random values, including passwords, password reset tokens, and activation keys, are generated using a pseudo-random number generator that is not cryptographically secure. An attacker who obtains a single generated value can reverse-engineer the internal PRNG state and predict all past and future token values, enabling targeted account takeover.

L3.area: Server-side random value generation utility template used for authentication tokens and user credentials
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where all security-sensitive random strings — including passwords, password reset tokens, activation keys, and session remember-me tokens — are generated using Apache Commons Lang's non-cryptographically-secure PRNG instead of a SecureRandom-backed source. An attacker who obtains a single generated token can reverse-engineer the internal PRNG state and predict all past and future token values.

ground_truth.reason: The application uses RandomStringUtils with its default non-cryptographically-secure PRNG to generate all security-sensitive random values including passwords, reset tokens, and activation keys, allowing an attacker who obtains one token to predict all past and future tokens.
